title: Map a Market You Know
description: Apply supply and demand thinking to a market from your everyday life.

steps.title: Choose a market
steps.instruction: Pick a market you interact with regularly: coffee shops in your city, ride-sharing, apartments in a neighborhood, concert tickets.
steps.prompt: What market are you analyzing? Who are the buyers? Who are the sellers?

steps.title: Identify demand factors
steps.instruction: What makes buyers want more or less of this good/service? List at least 3 demand factors.
steps.prompt: How would each factor shift demand? (e.g., 'If income rises, demand for restaurant meals increases')

steps.title: Identify supply factors
steps.instruction: What makes sellers willing to produce more or less? Think about costs, technology, number of sellers.
steps.prompt: List 3 supply factors. How would each shift supply?

steps.title: Find a recent price change
steps.instruction: Has the price in your chosen market changed recently? Try to explain it using supply and demand.
steps.prompt: Did supply shift, demand shift, or both? Draw a quick sketch of the curves and the shift if you can.

steps.title: Spot the opportunity cost
steps.instruction: What is the opportunity cost of buying in this market? What are you giving up?
steps.prompt: Write the opportunity cost for a typical buyer in this market.

steps.title: Identify a market failure (if any)
steps.instruction: Does your market have externalities? Public goods problems? Information asymmetry?
steps.prompt: Describe any market failure you see. Does anything prevent the market from being efficient?
